title: TDA for early detection of resistant-state transitions in cancer single-cell data
benchmark_goal: Test whether TDA-derived features improve early resistant-state detection beyond standard single-cell baselines on a concrete resistance-labeled benchmark.
falsification_rule: Reject the H4 claim if TDA features provide no measurable lift over standard single-cell trajectory or embedding baselines on the chosen resistance-related label.

dataset_routes.title: Melanoma targeted-therapy resistance-state benchmark

notes: Primary benchmark framing is binary resistant vs non-resistant.
A multi-state transition task remains optional and should not be the first MVP target.

blocking_issues: Verify which metadata columns in the raw files actually instantiate sample_id, condition_label, and replicate_id.
Confirm whether sample-group-level splitting is feasible without collapsing the benchmark to too few train/test groups.
Confirm whether the benchmark should be evaluated primarily at the sample-group level or only as a cell-level classification task.
notes: Fast executable benchmark route for H4.
Contains untreated or sensitive and single- or double-drug-resistant A375 melanoma states.
Best first route for testing resistant-state detection rather than broad clinical response.

dataset_routes.title: Melanoma checkpoint-immunotherapy response benchmark

notes: Response classification is clinically meaningful but not identical to an explicit resistant-state transition benchmark.

blocking_issues: Decide whether the target is patient response, cell-state program enrichment, or another resistance-adjacent label.
Verify that patient-level partitioning is sufficient to avoid leakage.
Verify whether this route should stay phase-2 or replace the default route after audit.
notes: Patient-derived follow-up route with clinically meaningful responder or non-responder labels.
Useful phase-2 benchmark after the fast GSE164897 scaffold.

dataset_routes.title: scCT-DB patient-derived longitudinal search layer

notes: This is a search layer, not a benchmark by itself.

blocking_issues: Choose one concrete patient-derived dataset with usable treatment and response metadata.
Verify whether a longitudinal transition benchmark with at least three timepoints is available and open.
notes: Phase-3 route for finding stronger patient-derived longitudinal benchmarks.
Not a single benchmark by itself; requires selecting one concrete dataset.

baselines.description: PCA or UMAP embeddings with clustering or simple state classifiers.
Pseudotime or transition-score baselines from standard single-cell workflows.
Persistence or related TDA-derived features over single-cell state geometry.
